questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The hyperbola $\frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$ passes through the point of intersection of the lines $x-3 \sqrt{5} y=0$ and $\sqrt{5} x-2 y=13$ and the length of its latus rectum is $4 / 3$ units. Then the value of $9 \mathrm{e}^2-1$ where $e$ is the eccentricity of the hyperbola is $\qquad$
questions.solution: The line $x-3 \sqrt{5} y=0$ and $\sqrt{5} x-2 y=13$ intersect at the point. Since the given hyperbola passes through this point, $\frac{(3 \sqrt{5})^2}{a^2} \frac{1}{b^2}=1$ or $\frac{45}{a^2}-\frac{1}{b^2}=1$ ....(1) Also length of the latus rectum $=\frac{2 b^2}{\mathrm{a}}=\frac{4}{3}$ (given) .....(2) From (1) and (2) we get $\frac{45}{a^2}-\frac{3}{2 a}=1$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{a}=6$ or $-\frac{15}{2}$ $\Rightarrow a\gt0$ therefore we have $a=6$ $\Rightarrow b^2=4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: The least possible area of convex quadrilateral having two vertices on two branches of hyperbola $x y=2$ and other two vertices on the two branches of $x y=-2$ is $\qquad$
questions.solution:
Minimum area will be in case of square with vertices
$(-\sqrt{2}, \sqrt{2}),(\sqrt{2}, \sqrt{2}),(\sqrt{2},-\sqrt{2})$ and $(-\sqrt{2},-\sqrt{2})$
$\therefore$ Minimum area $=8 \mathrm{~s}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A hyperbola has the equation $\frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$. A tangent and normal to the hyperbola is drawn at the same point. The tangent has $y$-intercept $\alpha$ and normal has $y$-intercept $\beta$. If $\alpha=-4$ and $\beta=9$ then find the $x$ coordinate of the focus $c$ of hyperbola. (Given $c$ lies on the positive $x$-axis.)
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{T}: \quad \frac{x}{a} \sec \theta-\frac{y}{b} \tan \theta=1 \\ & x=0 \Rightarrow y=-b \cot \theta=-4\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad b \cot \theta=4$ ....(1)
$\mathrm{N}: \quad \frac{a x}{\sec \theta}+\frac{b y}{\tan \theta}=a^2+b^2$
$x=0 ; y=\frac{a^2+b^2}{b} \tan \theta=9$ ...(2)
Eqn. (1) $\times$ Eqn. (2)
$$
\begin{aligned}
a^2+b^2 & =36=a^2 e^2 \\
a e & = \pm 6
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore x$-coordinate of $c$ is $a e=6$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.solution:
eliminating $m$ from given equations by mutliplying the above two equations we have
we get $\frac{\mathrm{x}^2}{(25)^2}-\frac{\mathrm{y}^2}{(24)^2}=1$, Radius of director circle $=\sqrt{a^2-b^2}$
clearly its director circle will have radius $=7$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the tangent on the point $(3 \sec \phi, 4 \tan \phi)$ (which is in first quadrant) of the hyperbola $\frac{x^2}{9}-\frac{y^2}{16}=1$ is perpendicular to $3 x+8 y-12=0$, then the value of $\phi$ is (in degree)
questions.solution:
Tangent on $(3 \sec \phi, 4 \tan \phi)$ is
$\frac{\sec \phi}{3} x-\frac{\tan \phi}{4} y=1$
given that (i) is $\perp$ to $3 x+8 y-12=0$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \frac{4}{3}\left(\frac{\sec \phi}{\tan \phi}\right)\left(\frac{-3}{8}\right)=-1 \\
& \Rightarrow \phi=30^{\circ}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $C$ the centre of the hyperbola $\frac{x^2}{9}-\frac{y^2}{16}=1$. The tangents at any point $P$ on this hyperbola meets the striaght lines $4 x-3 y=0$ and $4 x+3 y=0$ in the points $Q$ and $R$ respectively. Then $C Q . C R=$
questions.solution:
$P$ is $(3 \sec \theta, 4 \tan \theta)$
Tangent at $P$ is $\frac{x}{3} \sec \theta-\frac{y}{4} \tan \theta=1$

It meets $4 x-3 y=0$, i.e. $\frac{x}{3}=\frac{y}{4}$ in $Q$
$\therefore \quad Q$ is $\left(\frac{3}{\sec \theta-\tan \theta}, \frac{4}{\sec \theta-\tan \theta}\right)$

It meets $4 x+3 y=0$, i.e. $\frac{x}{3}=-\frac{y}{4}$ in $R$
$\therefore \quad \mathrm{R}$ is $\left(\frac{3}{\sec \theta+\tan \theta}, \frac{-4}{\sec \theta+\tan \theta}\right)$
$\therefore \quad C Q . C R=\left(\frac{\sqrt{3^2+4^2}}{\sec \theta-\tan \theta}\right)\left(\frac{\sqrt{3^2+4^2}}{\sec \theta+\tan \theta}\right)=25$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The number of normal(s) to hyperbola which can touch its conjugate is equal to
questions.solution:
Normal to hyperbola at t is $\mathrm{y}-\frac{\mathrm{c}}{\mathrm{t}}=\mathrm{t}^2(\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{ct})$ $\qquad$  ...(1)
It touches $x y=-c^2$
Solving with (1), we get $x\left\{\frac{c}{t}+t^2(x-c t)\right\}+c^2=0 \Rightarrow t^2 x^2+\left(\frac{c}{t}-c t^3\right) x+c^2=0$
For equal roots, we have $\left(\frac{1}{t}-t^3\right)^2-4 t^2=0 \Rightarrow\left(1-t^4\right)^2-4 t^4=0$ $\Rightarrow t^4-1-2 t^2=0$ or $t^4-1+2 t^2=0$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{t}^2=\frac{2 \pm \sqrt{8}}{2}$ or $\frac{-2 \pm \sqrt{8}}{2} \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}^2=1+\sqrt{2}$ or $-1+\sqrt{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $H: x^2+y^2+4 x y+8 x+8 y+8=0$ be a hyperbola. A line $L: x+y+1=0$ intersects the hyperbola $H$ at two distinct points. If radius of the circle which touches the hyperbola at the points where $H$ meets the line $L$ is $R$, then find the value of $R^2$.
questions.solution:
Solving $\mathrm{H}$ and $\mathrm{L}$ we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
2 x^2+2 x-1 & =\langle\beta \\
\alpha+\beta & =-1 ...(1)\\
\alpha \beta & =\frac{-1}{2} ...(2)
\end{aligned}
$$
Eqn. of family of circles passing through $A$ and $B$ is $S_D+\lambda_L=0$
$$
\begin{gathered}
(x-\alpha)(x-\beta)+(y+\alpha+1)(y+\beta+1)+\lambda(x+y+1)=0 \\
x^2+y^2-(\alpha+\beta) x+\alpha \beta+(\alpha+\beta+2) y+(\alpha+1)(\beta+1)+\lambda(x+y+1)=0 \\
x^2+y^2+x-\frac{1}{2}+y-\frac{1}{2}+1(x+y+1)=0
\end{gathered}
$$
$\therefore$ Finally of circles through $A$ and $B$ is
$$
x^2+y^2+x(\lambda+1)+y(\lambda+1)+\lambda-1=0
$$

Now, $\therefore$ this circle touches at $A(\alpha-\alpha-1)$

$$
\left.\therefore \quad \frac{d y}{d x}\right]_A \times m_{A C}=-1
$$

Diff. hyperbola at $A$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2 x+2 y \frac{d y}{d x}+4\left[\frac{x d y}{d x}+y\right]+8+8 \frac{d y}{d x}=0 \\
& \frac{d y}{d x}[y+2 x+4]+x+2 y+4=0 \\
& \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{-(x+2 y+4)}{2 x+y+4}
\end{aligned}
$$

Put $x=\alpha \quad y=-\alpha-1$
$$
\left.\frac{d y}{d x}\right]_A=\frac{\alpha-2}{\alpha+3}
$$
Let centre of circle is $\left\{\frac{-(\lambda+1)}{2}, \frac{-(\lambda+1)}{2}\right\}=\{k, k\}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
m_{A C} & =\frac{k+\alpha+1}{k-\alpha} \\
\therefore \quad m_1 m_2 & =-1 \\
\frac{\alpha-2}{\alpha+3} \times \frac{k+\alpha+1}{k-\alpha} & =-1 \\
\frac{\alpha-2}{\alpha+3} & =\frac{-(k-\alpha)}{k+\alpha+1} \\
\frac{1+2 \alpha}{-5} & =\frac{-k+\alpha+k+\alpha+1}{-k+\alpha-k-\alpha-1} \\
\frac{2 \alpha+1}{-5} & =\frac{2 \alpha+1}{-2 k-1} \\
\alpha \quad & \neq-\frac{1}{2}[From Eqn. (1) \& (2)]
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
2 k+1 & =5 \\
k & =2 \\
\therefore \quad \frac{-(\lambda+1)}{2} & =2 \Rightarrow \lambda=-5
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore$ Eqn. of circle is $x^2+y^2-4 x-4 y-6=0$
$$
R^2=4+4+6=14
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
